subject: Single MX-style mechanical switch in side-profile cross-section, three labelled anatomy layers stacked vertically — top housing (PC) with an LED window and mount-clip nub plus a faint inner-highlight stroke suggesting slight transparency; central cruciform stem mid-travel with two leader lines pointing to the rail surfaces where lube pools; bottom housing (nylon) with stippled hatching along its outer walls suggesting matte opacity, a thin metal leaf contact inside, two through-hole pin housings beneath the floor, and a quick zigzag spring coil filling the central cavity.

palette.background: transparent (sits cleanly on the dark-mode charcoal #1d1f24 and on light paper)

motif: MX-style switch cross-section, PC top vs nylon bottom labelled
notes: Single-splash warm-ochre line drawing — material contrast carried by surface texture, not by separate cross-sections: top housing reads as PC via a faint inner-highlight stroke (transparency cue), bottom housing reads as nylon via short staggered stipple ticks along its outer walls (matte/grippy cue). Stem rail-lube leaders fan out left with a small mono caption; anatomy labels (PC top, stem, nylon bottom) sit to the right with thin leader lines. No interior fills, family-convention 2 px stroke weight, brass-bronze ornamental dot at upper-right per the hero set.
